title: Yarin

{{Quote|Will you tell this… this tree stump here, who seems to have wood for brains, that I need to get a submersible, and I need it now?|Anja Gallandro|<ref name="CCR" />}}
The '''Yarin''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] whose appearance resembled that of [[Tree/Legends|trees]]; their [[Limb/Legends|limbs]] were reminiscent of branches, while their skin had a [[Wood/Legends|wooden]] texture. The species was mobile but slow moving. In [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]], a [[Yarin Harbormaster|Yarin]] served as [[Harbormaster/Legends|Harbormaster]] on the [[Dac/Legends|Dac]] resort known as [[Crystal Reef]]. He loaned his personal [[Mon Calamari submersible|submersible]] to the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Cilghal]] and her companions from the [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] [[Jedi Praxeum]].

==Biology and appearance==
The Yarin were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] that resembled [[Tree/Legends|trees]], with skin that looked like [[Wood/Legends|wood]]. They had branch-like [[Limb/Legends|arms]] and branching hands, as well as root-feet that they sometimes dangled in [[Water/Legends|water]]. They were slow moving and spoke ponderously, occasionally swaying on the spot.<ref name="CCR" />

==Society and culture==
The Yarin had their own [[Yarin language|language]], in which the word "''elfa''" meant "fish so small, it's not worth catching,"<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|229|Elfa}}</ref> and they could also speak [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]]. Their laughter sounded like wheezing to [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Ear/Legends|ears]].<ref name="CCR" />

==History==
{{Quote|And how may I make your stay at Crystal Reef more enjoyable?|The Yarin Harbormaster, to Cilghal|<ref name="CCR" />}}
The Yarin were active in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]<ref name="CCR" /> by at least [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]].<ref name="Date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Young Jedi Knights: Crisis at Crystal Reef]]'' to [[24 ABY/Legends|24 ABY]].</ref> In that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Yarin Harbormaster|Yarin]] served as the [[Harbormaster/Legends|Harbormaster]] at the [[Crystal Reef]] resort on the [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]] world of [[Dac/Legends|Dac]]. He was responsible for hiring out [[Mon Calamari submersible|submersibles]] and other watercraft to the tourists at the resort. On one occasion he was approached by a [[Human/Legends|Human]] girl [[Anja Gallandro]], who wanted to use a submersible to travel under the polar icecaps and destroy a stash of [[Andris/Legends|andris]] [[Spice/Legends|spice]] being stored there by the criminal [[Czethros]].<ref name="CCR" />

While the girl was unable to secure one on her own as she had no booking, the Yarin gladly lent his personal submersible, ''[[Elfa]]'', to the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and [[Ambassador/Legends|Ambassador]] [[Cilghal]], impressed by her status and position. She was also accompanied by the [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi trainee]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], [[Tenel Ka Djo|Tenel Ka]] and [[Zekk]], who were friends with Gallandro. Together, they were able to destroy the andris spice, encountering a rare [[Great Arctic Skra'akan]] along the way, which attacked the minisub. When the Jedi team returned to the Yarin, the ''Elfa'' had sustained considerable damage. Nevertheless, the Yarin was far more concerned with their own well-being than the condition of his craft. Their encounter with the Skra'akan, however, excited him, as he believed that experience conveyed good [[luck]]. He hoped his craft might have captured some of the encounter on [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]] so that he could display the footage at Crystal Reef.<ref name="CCR" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:SentientTrees-PE.png|thumb|right|200px|A treelike sentient from ''Star Wars: Ewoks'']]
The Yarin were created by [[Kevin J. Anderson]] and [[Rebecca Moesta]] for their young adults reader ''[[Young Jedi Knights: Crisis at Crystal Reef]]'', published in [[1998]].<ref name="CCR" /> A similar individual appears in their earlier book ''[[Young Jedi Knights: Jedi Under Siege]]'', described as "one of Master Skywalker's Jedi, a slow-moving, long-lived plantlike creature" and as a "treelike Jedi."<ref name="JUS">''[[Young Jedi Knights: Jedi Under Siege]]''</ref> Another [[Unidentified talking tree species|talking tree]] appears in "[[Party Ewok]]," an episode of [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']].<ref name="PE">{{Ewoks|Party Ewok}}</ref>
